description: Steering examples for the deal-desk-watcher orchestrator and its three subagents. Each example demonstrates operator intent, expected subagent behavior, and guardrail enforcement.

examples.label: Full breach scan — all records confirmed
examples.scenario: Operator runs the watcher. HubSpot returns three breached deals (one Critical, one High, one Medium). Operator confirms all log writes. Digest posts to Slack.
orchestrator_behavior: Reads company profile from ~/.cs-agent/practice-profile.json
Sends config payload to deal-stage-reader
Receives 3 breaches: one approval_aging Critical, one stage_sla High, one single_threaded_late_stage Medium
Invokes sla-log-writer with require_confirmation: true
After operator confirms all, receives write_status=completed, records_written=3
Invokes deal-alert-poster with full breach list and write_summary
Reports completion summary

subagent_behaviors.deal-stage-reader: Pulls all open deals, filters out Closed-Won/Closed-Lost, computes time-in-stage and approval age, returns sorted breach list with scan_metadata
subagent_behaviors.sla-log-writer: Renders proposal table with all 3 records, pauses for confirmation, appends all 3 records to ~/.cs-agent/deal-desk-sla-log.json after operator responds 'confirm all', returns write receipt with write_log
subagent_behaviors.deal-alert-poster: Formats digest with 🔴 Critical row first, 🟠 High second, 🟡 Medium third. Posts to #revops-alignment. Returns delivery_status=posted.

guardrails_enforced: G8 (owner + age + breach type on every row)

examples.label: Partial log write — operator confirms subset
examples.scenario: Two Critical breaches found. Operator confirms only the first record for logging. The second is skipped. Digest still posts both breach rows.
orchestrator_behavior: deal-stage-reader returns 2 Critical breaches
sla-log-writer pauses with 2-row proposal table
Operator confirms record 1 only
sla-log-writer writes record 1, skips record 2, returns write_status=partial, records_written=1, records_skipped=1
deal-alert-poster receives both breach rows (unfiltered) and write_summary with status=partial
Digest footer shows: '1 record(s) written, 1 skipped by operator'
examples.key_behavior: Log write scope (partial) does not filter the breach rows posted to Slack. Both deals are surfaced in the digest regardless of log write decision.

examples.label: Operator declines all log writes
examples.scenario: Three breaches found. Operator responds 'skip all' to the write proposal. SLA log is not written. Digest still posts.
orchestrator_behavior: sla-log-writer receives skip all response
Returns write_status=skipped, records_written=0, records_skipped=3
write_log contains skipped entries with human_approval_status=skipped for all 3
Orchestrator sets write_summary.status=skipped and passes to deal-alert-poster
Digest footer shows: 'Log write skipped by operator — no records written'
Digest breach rows are unaffected — all 3 deals surface in Slack
examples.key_behavior: Operator declining writes is a valid governance outcome. Downstream digest delivery proceeds normally. The watcher never treats skipped writes as errors.

examples.label: No breaches — clean pipeline
examples.scenario: HubSpot returns 12 open deals, none exceeding any SLA threshold. No subagents downstream of deal-stage-reader are invoked for write or alert.
orchestrator_behavior: deal-stage-reader evaluates all 12 deals, returns breaches=[], deals_evaluated=12
Orchestrator skips sla-log-writer (breaches empty): write_status=not_triggered, records_written=0
Invokes deal-alert-poster with empty breach array
deal-alert-poster formats no-breach digest: '✅ No SLA breaches detected.'
Posts to #revops-alignment
Completion summary: Breaches found: 0, Log entries: 0 (not_triggered)
examples.key_behavior: sla-log-writer is not invoked when breaches is empty — the orchestrator sets write_status=not_triggered without calling the subagent.
guardrails_enforced: G6 (freshness still shown in digest even with no breaches)

examples.label: HubSpot unavailable — hard stop
examples.scenario: deal-stage-reader cannot reach HubSpot. Orchestrator hard-stops. Neither sla-log-writer nor deal-alert-poster is invoked.
orchestrator_behavior: deal-stage-reader returns { 'error': 'hubspot_unavailable', 'message': 'Cannot evaluate deals without CRM data.' }
Orchestrator stops immediately
Reports to operator: 'Deal Desk Watcher — cannot run. HubSpot unavailable.'
sla-log-writer NOT invoked
deal-alert-poster NOT invoked
examples.key_behavior: This is the same hard-stop pattern used in churn-signal-scanner. No partial run — if CRM data is unavailable, no breach evaluation can be trusted, so the entire run aborts.
guardrails_enforced: No Slack noise on incomplete data

examples.label: Filesystem write failure — digest still posts
examples.scenario: sla-log-writer fails to write to ~/.cs-agent/deal-desk-sla-log.json (permission error). Orchestrator continues to deal-alert-poster with write_status=failed.
orchestrator_behavior: sla-log-writer returns write_status=failed, error=filesystem_write_failed, records_written=0
Orchestrator does NOT stop — routes failure to deal-alert-poster via write_summary
deal-alert-poster formats digest footer: '⚠️ SLA log write failed — manual logging required at ~/.cs-agent/deal-desk-sla-log.json'
Posts digest to Slack with failure noted
Completion summary shows write failure clearly
examples.key_behavior: Filesystem failures are surfaced, not silent. Downstream Slack delivery still proceeds — the RevOps team needs visibility even if local logging is broken.

examples.label: Preview mode — digest rendered but not posted
examples.scenario: Operator passes preview_mode=true. deal-alert-poster formats the full digest but suppresses Slack delivery.
examples.operator_trigger: Run deal desk watcher in preview mode.
orchestrator_behavior: Passes preview_mode: true in deal-alert-poster payload
deal-alert-poster formats full digest, sets delivery_status=preview, channels_posted=[]
alert_preview is populated with full text
Orchestrator surfaces digest in-session
Completion summary shows: 'Posted to: Preview only'
examples.key_behavior: Preview mode allows RevOps to validate digest formatting before enabling the scheduled run. All breach data, severity rows, and guardrail footers are rendered exactly as they would appear in Slack.
guardrails_enforced: G8 enforced even in preview — invalid rows would still be caught

examples.label: Mixed severity breaches — sort order enforced
examples.scenario: Five deals in breach: 1 Critical (approval_aging, $210k ACV), 1 Critical (stage_sla 2×+ overage, $95k ACV), 2 High (stage_sla and close_date_drift, $150k and $80k ACV), 1 Medium (single_threaded, $320k ACV). Digest must sort Critical first by ACV desc, then High by ACV desc, then Medium.
examples.key_behavior: The $320k Medium-severity deal sorts BELOW the $80k High-severity deal. Severity tier takes precedence over ACV. This prevents high-probability, high-ACV but low-urgency deals from dominating the digest.
guardrails_enforced: G8 — each row includes owner, days/hours, breach type

examples.label: Unevaluable signals — partial breach record
examples.scenario: A deal is missing stage_entered_date in HubSpot. deal-stage-reader marks the stage_sla signal unevaluable for that deal but still evaluates the other three breach types.
deal_stage_reader_behavior: Encounters deal where stage_entered_date is null
Sets stage_sla breach_detail.unevaluable = true for that deal
Evaluates approval_aging, close_date_drift, and single_threaded_late_stage normally
Includes unevaluable_signals: ['stage_sla'] in the deal's output
Does NOT fabricate a stage_sla value
examples.deal_alert_poster_behavior: Renders: 'stage_sla: unevaluable (field missing from HubSpot)' in the breach row instead of computed days_in_stage
examples.key_behavior: Partial data never blocks the rest of the evaluation. Missing fields are surfaced explicitly in the digest so the RevOps team knows which signals were not assessed — they can check HubSpot directly for the missing field.
guardrails_enforced: G8 still met for signals that were evaluable
